topics.title: El Clasico: Real Madrid vs Barcelona

hook.text: Two cities, two identities, one football match that stops the world. El Clasico is sport's biggest rivalry.

buildup.text: Real Madrid represents Spanish centralism. Barcelona represents Catalan pride. The rivalry is political as much as sporting.

discovery.text: Franco's dictatorship weaponized Real Madrid as a symbol of Spanish unity, deepening Barcelona's defiance.

twist.text: Messi and Ronaldo elevated El Clasico into a personal duel. Their era produced the greatest football ever played.

climax.text: Over 650 million people watch each El Clasico. It transcends sport into culture, politics, and identity.

punchline.text: More than a game. Two cities fighting for Spain's soul through football.

quiz.question: What makes El Clasico more than just a football match?
options: It carries deep political and cultural significance between Madrid and Catalonia
It determines the league champion every year
It is the oldest football rivalry in the world

topics.title: Manchester United vs Liverpool

hook.text: England's two most successful clubs from rival industrial cities. The hatred runs deeper than football.

buildup.text: Manchester and Liverpool competed for trade dominance in the 19th century. The Ship Canal enraged Liverpool.

discovery.text: The rivalry intensified in the 1960s when both clubs dominated English football simultaneously.

twist.text: United won 13 Premier League titles while Liverpool waited 30 years for one. The power balance shifted dramatically.

climax.text: When Liverpool finally won the league in 2020, the celebration—and United fans' despair—was three decades deep.

punchline.text: Two cities built by industry, divided by football, united by mutual loathing.

quiz.question: What is the origin of the Manchester United vs Liverpool rivalry?
options: 19th-century industrial and trade competition between the two cities
A disputed transfer of a famous player
They share the same stadium

topics.title: Boca Juniors vs River Plate

hook.text: The 'Superclasico' is the most intense derby on Earth. Buenos Aires splits in half for every match.

buildup.text: Both clubs were founded in the La Boca neighborhood. River moved uptown; Boca stayed working class.

discovery.text: Boca became the club of the people. River became the club of wealth. Class warfare played out on the pitch.

twist.text: In the 2018 Copa Libertadores final, River fans attacked the Boca bus. The second leg was moved to Madrid.

climax.text: Despite the violence, the Superclasico remains football's rawest expression of passion and tribal identity.

punchline.text: Two clubs, one neighborhood, an entire nation divided. Football at its wildest.

quiz.question: What class divide defines the Boca vs River Plate rivalry?
options: Boca represents the working class; River represents wealth
Boca is from the north; River from the south
One club plays in a higher league than the other

topics.title: Celtic vs Rangers: The Old Firm

hook.text: Catholic vs Protestant. Irish vs British. Glasgow's Old Firm derby is sport's most religiously charged rivalry.

buildup.text: Celtic was founded in 1887 by an Irish Catholic community. Rangers became the club of Glasgow's Protestant majority.

discovery.text: Rangers had an unwritten policy of not signing Catholic players until 1989. The religious divide was institutional.

twist.text: Old Firm matches have been linked to spikes in domestic violence and sectarian incidents across Glasgow.

climax.text: Despite social progress, the rivalry remains Scotland's defining sporting event—fierce, tribal, and emotional.

punchline.text: Religion, identity, and 90 minutes of football. Glasgow's deepest divide.

quiz.question: What social divide underpins the Celtic vs Rangers rivalry?
options: Religious division between Catholic and Protestant communities
A dispute over stadium ownership
Different football philosophies

topics.title: AC Milan vs Inter Milan: The Derby della Madonnina

hook.text: Same city, same stadium, total war. Milan and Inter share San Siro but share nothing else.

buildup.text: Inter was founded in 1908 by breakaway members of AC Milan who wanted the club open to foreign players.

discovery.text: Inter became the 'international' club of immigrants. Milan kept its elite, establishment identity.

twist.text: They literally share a stadium—playing home games in alternating weeks at the 75,000-seat San Siro.

climax.text: Combined, they have won 10 European Cups. The derby decides which half of Milan owns bragging rights.

punchline.text: Same stadium, different worlds. Milan's derby is a civil war at home.

quiz.question: Why do AC Milan and Inter share a stadium?
options: Both clubs use the San Siro, alternating home games
One club rents from the other
The city only permits one football stadium

topics.title: Messi vs Ronaldo

hook.text: For 15 years, two men dominated football so completely that the rest were playing for third place.

buildup.text: Lionel Messi: a magical dribbler from Argentina. Cristiano Ronaldo: a physical machine from Portugal.

discovery.text: Between them, 13 Ballon d'Or awards, 1,700+ goals, and records that may never be broken.

twist.text: Their rivalry was intensified by playing for Barcelona and Real Madrid—El Clasico became their personal arena.

climax.text: The debate over who is greater may never be settled. Both pushed the other to heights neither would have reached alone.

punchline.text: They needed each other to become the greatest. Rivalry as fuel.

quiz.question: How many combined Ballon d'Or awards did Messi and Ronaldo win?
options: Thirteen
Ten
Eight

topics.title: Federer vs Nadal vs Djokovic

hook.text: Three players shared 66 Grand Slam titles over 20 years. Tennis may never see anything like it again.

buildup.text: Federer brought elegance. Nadal brought ferocity. Djokovic brought relentless precision. Each countered the others.

discovery.text: Nadal dominated Federer on clay. Djokovic solved both on hard courts. Each had a surface and a nemesis.

twist.text: Their rivalry extended careers—each refused to retire while the others kept winning. Pride fueled longevity.

climax.text: Federer retired in 2022 with Nadal crying beside him. Even rivals can share genuine love.

punchline.text: Three men, 66 titles, one golden era. Tennis perfected rivalry.

quiz.question: How many combined Grand Slam titles did Federer, Nadal, and Djokovic win?
options: Sixty-six
Fifty
Forty

topics.title: Ali vs Frazier: The Fight of the Century

hook.text: Two undefeated heavyweight champions. One fight to decide the greatest boxer alive. March 8, 1971.

buildup.text: Ali was brash, political, and beloved by the anti-war movement. Frazier was blue-collar and relentless.

discovery.text: Frazier knocked Ali down in the 15th round and won by decision. It was Ali's first professional loss.

twist.text: Ali mocked Frazier mercilessly before the fight, calling him 'ugly' and a 'gorilla.' The cruelty haunted Frazier for life.

climax.text: They fought twice more, including the brutal 'Thrilla in Manila.' Both men were never the same after.

punchline.text: The greatest rivalry in boxing left both champions permanently damaged.

quiz.question: Who won the first Ali vs Frazier fight?
options: Frazier by unanimous decision
Ali by knockout
It was declared a draw

topics.title: Senna vs Prost: Speed and Fury

hook.text: Two Formula One teammates who hated each other. Their rivalry nearly killed them both—and it did kill one.

buildup.text: Ayrton Senna and Alain Prost were McLaren teammates in 1988-89. Senna was raw speed; Prost was calculated brilliance.

discovery.text: In 1989, Prost deliberately collided with Senna to clinch the championship. A year later, Senna did the same to Prost.

twist.text: Their mutual crashing at championship-deciding races scandalized Formula One and drew worldwide attention.

climax.text: Senna died at Imola in 1994. Prost was a pallbearer at his funeral. The hatred had long since turned to respect.

punchline.text: They crashed into each other for glory and cried at each other's loss.

quiz.question: What made the Senna vs Prost rivalry so extreme?
options: They deliberately crashed into each other in championship-deciding races
They competed for the same sponsor
They were from the same country

topics.title: Navratilova vs Evert: Tennis Opposites

hook.text: They played each other 80 times—more than any rivalry in tennis history. One attacked; one defended.

buildup.text: Martina Navratilova played serve-and-volley. Chris Evert played from the baseline. Polar opposite styles.

discovery.text: Over their careers, Navratilova led 43-37 in head-to-head. Evert dominated clay; Navratilova owned the grass.

twist.text: Off the court, they became close friends. Their rivalry was fierce but never bitter or personal.

climax.text: Together they elevated women's tennis into a major professional sport and inspired generations of female athletes.

punchline.text: Eighty matches. Opposite styles. Lifelong friends. The perfect rivalry.

quiz.question: How many times did Navratilova and Evert play each other?
options: 80 times
60 times
100 times

topics.title: India vs Pakistan: Cricket's Holy War

hook.text: When India plays Pakistan in cricket, a billion people stop everything. It's the most-watched rivalry on Earth.

buildup.text: Partition in 1947 split the subcontinent into two nations. Cricket became a proxy for unresolved tensions.

discovery.text: India vs Pakistan World Cup matches regularly draw over 1 billion viewers—the biggest audiences in sport.

twist.text: The two teams rarely play bilateral series due to political tensions. Major tournaments are their only battlefield.

climax.text: The 2011 World Cup semifinal in Mohali was attended by both prime ministers as a diplomatic gesture.

punchline.text: Two nations, one game, a billion hearts racing. Cricket's fiercest rivalry.

quiz.question: Why is the India vs Pakistan cricket rivalry so intense?
options: It reflects the political tensions from the 1947 Partition
They compete for the same trophy every year
Both countries only play cricket

topics.title: USA vs USSR: Cold War on the Court

hook.text: The 1972 Olympic basketball final had three seconds replayed three times. America lost—and still refuses to accept it.

buildup.text: The USA had never lost an Olympic basketball game. In 1972 at Munich, they faced the Soviet Union in the gold medal game.

discovery.text: With three seconds left, the Soviets scored. Officials controversially reset the clock twice, giving the USSR extra chances.

twist.text: The US team voted unanimously to refuse the silver medals. They remain unclaimed in a Swiss vault to this day.

climax.text: The game became a Cold War metaphor—capitalism vs communism, decided by three contested seconds.

punchline.text: Three seconds, three tries, and fifty years of American outrage.

quiz.question: What happened to the US team's silver medals from the 1972 Olympics?
options: The team refused them and they remain unclaimed in a vault
They were melted down
Each player donated theirs to charity

topics.title: The Ashes: England vs Australia in Cricket

hook.text: A tiny urn containing burnt cricket bails. Two nations have fought over it for 142 years.

buildup.text: After Australia beat England in 1882, a newspaper wrote that English cricket had died and 'the body will be cremated.'

discovery.text: Bails were burned and placed in an urn. The Ashes series was born—the oldest rivalry in international cricket.

twist.text: The actual urn is just 4 inches tall and stays permanently at Lord's. The teams play for a crystal replica.

climax.text: The 2005 Ashes series is considered the greatest cricket series ever played—England won by just 2 runs in one Test.

punchline.text: A 4-inch urn and 142 years of national pride. Cricket's oldest war.

quiz.question: What is the origin of 'The Ashes' name?
options: A newspaper obituary said English cricket had died and would be cremated
The first match was played near an ash tree
The trophy was rescued from a fire

topics.title: All Blacks vs Springboks

hook.text: New Zealand's haka meets South Africa's physicality. Rugby's greatest rivalry spans over a century.

buildup.text: The All Blacks and Springboks first played in 1921. Their contests became rugby's gold standard for intensity.

discovery.text: During apartheid, New Zealand's 1981 tour of South Africa caused massive protests across both countries.

twist.text: In 1995, Nelson Mandela wore a Springbok jersey to the World Cup final—turning a symbol of oppression into one of unity.

climax.text: South Africa's 2023 World Cup win over New Zealand proved the rivalry remains the pinnacle of rugby.

punchline.text: Haka meets Springbok. Rugby's fiercest collision on and off the field.

quiz.question: What made the All Blacks vs Springboks rivalry political?
options: Tours during apartheid caused massive protests in both countries
The two countries disputed a player's nationality
They disagreed on rugby rule changes

topics.title: Miracle on Ice: USA vs USSR Hockey

hook.text: A team of college kids beat the best hockey team in the world. 'Do you believe in miracles? Yes!'

buildup.text: The 1980 Soviet hockey team had won four consecutive Olympic gold medals. They were unbeatable professionals.

discovery.text: US coach Herb Brooks assembled a team of amateur college players and drilled them with punishing preparation.

twist.text: The Americans beat the Soviets 4-3 in a semifinal, not the gold medal game. They still had to beat Finland for gold.

climax.text: Al Michaels' call—'Do you believe in miracles?'—became one of the most famous moments in American sports history.

punchline.text: College kids beat the Soviet empire on ice. America's greatest upset.

quiz.question: What was surprising about the US hockey team in 1980?
options: They were amateur college players who beat professionals
They had never played together before the tournament
Their coach was from the Soviet Union

topics.title: Lakers vs Celtics: Basketball's Epic War

hook.text: Purple and gold vs green and white. Lakers vs Celtics is the NBA's most storied and bitter rivalry.

buildup.text: They have met in the NBA Finals 12 times—more than any other pairing. Boston leads the series 9-3.

discovery.text: The 1980s rivalry between Magic Johnson and Larry Bird revived the NBA from near-irrelevance.

twist.text: Magic and Bird started as fierce enemies. They became best friends—even doing TV commercials together.

climax.text: Their rivalry is credited with saving the NBA. Commissioner David Stern called them 'the two who made it happen.'

punchline.text: Two players who hated each other ended up saving the sport they loved.

quiz.question: How many times have the Lakers and Celtics met in the NBA Finals?
options: Twelve times
Eight times
Fifteen times

topics.title: Canadiens vs Bruins: Hockey's Original Hatred

hook.text: French Canada vs English America. The Montreal Canadiens and Boston Bruins rivalry is hockey's oldest fire.

buildup.text: They first played in 1924. The Canadiens represent French-Canadian identity; the Bruins embody tough American hockey.

discovery.text: The two teams have met in 34 playoff series—more than any other NHL pairing—with Montreal leading 25-9.

twist.text: The 1979 'Too Many Men on the Ice' penalty against Boston led to a Canadiens goal and series win—still debated.

climax.text: Even as both teams' fortunes have fluctuated, the hatred between their fan bases remains as fierce as ever.

punchline.text: French pride vs Boston grit. A century of hockey hatred.

quiz.question: What cultural divide fuels the Canadiens vs Bruins rivalry?
options: French-Canadian identity vs American toughness
Both teams compete for the same local fans
A disputed trade in the 1950s

topics.title: Pistons vs Bulls: The Jordan Rules

hook.text: The Detroit Pistons created a defensive playbook specifically to stop Michael Jordan. They called it 'The Jordan Rules.'

buildup.text: The 'Bad Boy' Pistons physically punished Jordan every time he drove to the basket—elbows, shoves, and hard fouls.

discovery.text: The Pistons eliminated the Bulls three consecutive years from 1988 to 1990 with aggressive, bruising defense.

twist.text: Jordan used the losses as motivation. He added muscle and developed a mid-range game to counter the physicality.

climax.text: In 1991, the Bulls swept the Pistons. Detroit walked off the court refusing to shake hands. The era had ended.

punchline.text: They built rules to stop him. He changed his game and destroyed them.

quiz.question: What were 'The Jordan Rules'?
options: The Pistons' defensive playbook designed to physically stop Jordan
NBA regulations limiting one player's scoring
A training program Jordan followed

topics.title: Yankees vs Red Sox: Baseball's Blood Feud

hook.text: An 86-year curse, a sold player, and two cities that genuinely despise each other. Baseball's greatest rivalry.

buildup.text: In 1920, the Red Sox sold Babe Ruth to the Yankees. Boston didn't win another World Series for 86 years.

discovery.text: 'The Curse of the Bambino' haunted Boston. The Yankees won 26 championships while the Red Sox suffered.

twist.text: In 2004, Boston came back from 3-0 down against the Yankees in the ALCS—the only team ever to do so.

climax.text: The Red Sox won the World Series that year, finally ending the curse. But the rivalry burns as hot as ever.

punchline.text: They sold Babe Ruth and paid for it for 86 years. That's the curse.

quiz.question: What was the 'Curse of the Bambino'?
options: An 86-year championship drought after the Red Sox sold Babe Ruth to the Yankees
A banned pitch used in the 1920 World Series
A jinxed baseball stadium

topics.title: Tonya Harding vs Nancy Kerrigan

hook.text: A figure skater was attacked before the Olympics. Her rival's ex-husband organized the assault.

buildup.text: In January 1994, Nancy Kerrigan was struck on the knee by an assailant after a practice session.

discovery.text: The attack was linked to Tonya Harding's ex-husband and bodyguard. Harding claimed she knew nothing beforehand.

twist.text: Both skated at the 1994 Olympics. Kerrigan won silver; Harding finished eighth after her lace broke on the ice.

climax.text: The scandal became the most-watched sporting event of 1994 and turned figure skating into tabloid entertainment.

punchline.text: An attack on ice turned a rivalry into the biggest scandal in Olympic history.

quiz.question: What happened to Nancy Kerrigan before the 1994 Olympics?
options: She was physically attacked on the knee in a planned assault
She was disqualified for a rule violation
She suffered an accidental injury during practice

topics.title: The Iron Bowl: Alabama vs Auburn

hook.text: Families refuse to speak to each other on game day. In Alabama, college football rivalry is personal.

buildup.text: The University of Alabama and Auburn University are separated by 160 miles of pure football obsession.

discovery.text: The rivalry was so intense that the two schools refused to play each other from 1907 to 1948—a 41-year gap.

twist.text: In 2013, Auburn's Chris Davis returned a missed field goal 109 yards for a touchdown on the final play—the 'Kick Six.'

climax.text: The Iron Bowl shapes recruiting, politics, and social life across the entire state of Alabama.

punchline.text: One state, two teams, zero neutrality. The Iron Bowl divides Alabama.

quiz.question: Why didn't Alabama and Auburn play each other from 1907 to 1948?
options: The rivalry was so intense the schools refused to schedule the game
One school dropped its football program
A natural disaster prevented travel between the schools

topics.title: Oxford vs Cambridge: The Boat Race

hook.text: Two elite universities race rowing boats on the Thames. It's been happening since 1829 and millions still watch.

buildup.text: The Boat Race is a 4.2-mile course on the Thames between Putney and Mortlake in London.

discovery.text: Cambridge leads overall wins, but the margins are often agonizingly close—sometimes less than a boat length.

twist.text: In 2012, a protester swam into the path of the boats, forcing a restart. The disruption nearly caused a collision.

climax.text: The race attracts 250,000 spectators along the Thames and millions of TV viewers worldwide despite being amateur.

punchline.text: Two schools, one river, almost 200 years of bragging rights.

quiz.question: How long has the Oxford vs Cambridge Boat Race been running?
options: Since 1829
Since 1900
Since 1750

topics.title: Michigan vs Ohio State: The Game

hook.text: They call it simply 'The Game.' Michigan vs Ohio State is American college football's most iconic rivalry.

buildup.text: The rivalry dates to 1897. Ohio State coach Woody Hayes once refused to buy gas in Michigan—he'd rather run out.

discovery.text: The 1969 game—when Michigan upset undefeated Ohio State—is called 'The Upset of the Century' in college football.

twist.text: In 1973, both teams were 10-0 going into The Game. The tie sent Ohio State to the Rose Bowl, enraging Michigan.

climax.text: The rivalry defines the Big Ten conference and remains the most-anticipated regular-season game in college football.

punchline.text: Woody Hayes wouldn't even buy gas in Michigan. That's how deep it goes.

quiz.question: Why is Michigan vs Ohio State called 'The Game'?
options: It's considered the most important and iconic college football rivalry
It was the first college football game ever played
The winner receives a special trophy called 'The Game'

topics.title: North London Derby: Arsenal vs Tottenham

hook.text: Four miles apart in north London. Arsenal and Tottenham share a neighborhood but not a kind word.

buildup.text: Arsenal moved to north London in 1913—into Tottenham's territory. Spurs have resented them ever since.

discovery.text: Arsenal's invincible 2003-04 season—going unbeaten all league—included a victory at White Hart Lane that stung Spurs deeply.

twist.text: Tottenham's legendary trophy drought—'Lads, it's Tottenham'—became a meme that Arsenal fans use relentlessly.

climax.text: The north London derby regularly delivers drama: red cards, last-minute winners, and atmosphere that shakes the ground.

punchline.text: Four miles, 110 years, and still no peace in north London.

quiz.question: Why does Tottenham resent Arsenal?
options: Arsenal moved into north London—Tottenham's territory—in 1913
Arsenal was founded by former Tottenham players
They compete for the same stadium

topics.title: Duke vs North Carolina: Basketball's Best Feud

hook.text: Eight miles apart in North Carolina. Two basketball programs that have produced more legends than any other.

buildup.text: Duke and UNC have combined for 11 NCAA championships. The talent pipeline is unmatched in college basketball.

discovery.text: Coach K at Duke and Dean Smith at UNC built empires. Michael Jordan played for UNC; Grant Hill played for Duke.

twist.text: In 2022, they met in the Final Four for the first time ever—Coach K's final season. UNC won and ended his career.

climax.text: Tobacco Road's basketball rivalry defines March Madness and remains the purest expression of college sports.

punchline.text: Eight miles apart, eleven titles between them. College basketball's greatest feud.

quiz.question: What happened in the 2022 Duke vs UNC Final Four game?
options: UNC won, ending Coach K's coaching career
Duke won its sixth national title
The game went to triple overtime

topics.title: Hamilton vs Verstappen: F1's New War

hook.text: The 2021 F1 season came down to the last lap of the last race. One decision changed the champion.

buildup.text: Lewis Hamilton and Max Verstappen traded wins all season. They collided multiple times—each blaming the other.

discovery.text: At Abu Dhabi, a controversial safety car restart gave Verstappen fresh tires for one final lap. He overtook Hamilton and won.

twist.text: The race director's decision was so disputed that he was removed from his position. Hamilton was devastated.

climax.text: It was the most dramatic F1 season in decades—two generations colliding at 200 mph for the ultimate prize.

punchline.text: One lap, one pass, one controversy. F1's most dramatic finish ever.

quiz.question: Why was the 2021 Abu Dhabi F1 finale controversial?
options: A disputed safety car restart gave Verstappen fresh tires for the final lap
Hamilton's car was disqualified after the race
Both drivers crashed on the last lap

topics.title: Barcelona vs Bayern Munich in Europe

hook.text: Barcelona 2, Bayern Munich 8. The most humiliating result in Champions League history broke a dynasty.

buildup.text: Barcelona and Bayern Munich had been Europe's two strongest clubs for years—meeting repeatedly in the knockout rounds.

discovery.text: In the 2020 Champions League quarterfinal, Bayern destroyed Barcelona 8-2. Messi stood on the pitch in disbelief.

twist.text: That result ended Barcelona's era. The club spiraled into financial crisis and Messi was forced to leave.

climax.text: The fixture became a symbol of Barcelona's decline and Bayern's ruthless European dominance.

punchline.text: Eight goals in one night ended an empire. Football can be cruel.

quiz.question: What was the score in the infamous 2020 Barcelona vs Bayern Champions League match?
options: Bayern Munich 8, Barcelona 2
Bayern Munich 5, Barcelona 1
Barcelona 4, Bayern Munich 4

topics.title: Rivalry Reignited: Fury vs Usyk

hook.text: Two undefeated champions fighting to become the first undisputed heavyweight king in 25 years.

buildup.text: Tyson Fury held the WBC belt. Oleksandr Usyk held the WBA, IBF, and WBO. All four titles on the line.

discovery.text: Usyk won their first fight by split decision in May 2024—becoming the undisputed heavyweight champion.

twist.text: Usyk, a former cruiserweight from war-torn Ukraine, beat the larger Fury with skill, heart, and tactical brilliance.

climax.text: Their rematch kept the boxing world riveted—proof that heavyweight rivalry still captures the public imagination.

punchline.text: The smaller fighter from a war zone became the undisputed king. Boxing loves an underdog.

quiz.question: What was historically significant about Usyk's victory over Fury?
options: He became the first undisputed heavyweight champion in 25 years
He won by knockout in the first round
It was the longest heavyweight fight in history

topics.title: Djokovic vs Everyone: The Lone Wolf

hook.text: Novak Djokovic has the most Grand Slam titles ever. He also has the fewest fans of any all-time great.

buildup.text: While Federer was loved and Nadal was admired, Djokovic arrived as the unwanted third man in their rivalry.

discovery.text: Crowds routinely cheered against him. At the 2021 US Open final, fans openly supported his opponent.

twist.text: Being the villain fueled him. Djokovic won 24 Grand Slams—more than Federer's 20 and Nadal's 22.

climax.text: His deportation from Australia in 2022 over vaccination rules only deepened the love-hate relationship with fans.

punchline.text: They booed him. He won anyway. The greatest of all time, on his own terms.

quiz.question: How many Grand Slam titles has Novak Djokovic won?

topics.title: LeBron vs Curry: The Modern NBA Rivalry

hook.text: They met in four consecutive NBA Finals from 2015 to 2018. It defined a generation of basketball.

buildup.text: LeBron's Cavaliers represented old-school power basketball. Curry's Warriors played revolutionary three-point ball.

discovery.text: The Warriors won three of four Finals. But LeBron's 2016 comeback from 3-1 down was the defining moment.

twist.text: Kevin Durant's arrival in Golden State tilted the balance. LeBron responded by forming his own superteam in LA.

climax.text: Their rivalry elevated NBA Finals ratings to historic heights and made basketball globally must-see television.

punchline.text: Four straight Finals. Two different philosophies. Basketball's modern golden age.

quiz.question: How many consecutive NBA Finals did LeBron and Curry contest?
